vulnerabilities.description: The parameter 'title' is vulnerable to error-based SQL injection. DB error 'you have an error in your sql syntax' was reflected in the response.
vulnerabilities.evidence: you have an error in your sql syntax
vulnerabilities.remediation:
1. Use parameterized queries or prepared statements.
2. Apply input validation and whitelist allowed characters.
3. Use an ORM with built-in protection.
4. Apply the principle of least privilege to DB accounts.
5. Enable a WAF to filter malicious input.

vulnerabilities.description: Form field 'title' at 'http://localhost:8080/sqli_1.php' is vulnerable to SQL injection. DB error 'you have an error in your sql syntax' found in response.
vulnerabilities.evidence: you have an error in your sql syntax
vulnerabilities.remediation:
1. Use parameterized queries or prepared statements.
2. Apply input validation and whitelist allowed characters.
3. Use an ORM with built-in protection.
4. Apply the principle of least privilege to DB accounts.
5. Enable a WAF to filter malicious input.

vulnerabilities.description: Parameter 'title' reflects user input unescaped, allowing script injection via URL.
vulnerabilities.remediation:
1. Encode all user-supplied output using context-aware encoding.
2. Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers.
3. Use HTTPOnly and Secure flags on session cookies.
4. Validate and sanitize all input server-side.
5. Use a template engine that auto-escapes by default.

vulnerabilities.remediation:
1. Encode all user-supplied output using context-aware encoding.
2. Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers.
3. Use HTTPOnly and Secure flags on session cookies.
4. Validate and sanitize all input server-side.
5. Use a template engine that auto-escapes by default.

vulnerabilities.description: The application accepts default credentials 'admin' / 'admin'. This allows unauthorized access without any exploitation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Consult OWASP guidelines for this vulnerability type.

vulnerabilities.description: The POST form at 'http://localhost:8080/password_change.php' (from 'http://localhost:8080/password_change.php') does not contain a CSRF token. Without a token, attackers can trick authenticated users into submitting this form from any website.
vulnerabilities.evidence: POST form fields: ['password_curr', 'password_new', 'password_conf'] — none match known CSRF token names
vulnerabilities.remediation: Consult OWASP guidelines for this vulnerability type.

vulnerabilities.description: The POST form at 'http://localhost:8080/user_extra.php' (from 'http://localhost:8080/user_extra.php') does not contain a CSRF token. Without a token, attackers can trick authenticated users into submitting this form from any website.
vulnerabilities.evidence: POST form fields: ['login', 'email', 'password', 'password_conf', 'secret', 'mail_activation'] — none match known CSRF token names
vulnerabilities.remediation: Consult OWASP guidelines for this vulnerability type.
